State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660            322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

February 25, 1983


Mr. John Carroll Naish
Bay Area Rapid Transit District
800 Madison Street
Oakland, CA  94607

                                                Re:  Your Request for Advice, Our
                                                     Advice No. A-83-041

Dear Mr. Naish:

     Thank you for your request for advice on Government Code
Section 84308* (AB 1040).  Your question is whether, and to what
extent, the provisions of this section apply to the members of
the Board of Directors of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District.

     Section 84308(d) exempts "legislative bodies ... while acting
as a body or committee thereof" from the coverage of this
section.  A commission or board qualifies as a "legislative body"
only if its entire membership is directly elected by the voters to
serve on that body.  (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(a).)  The
term "voters" is defined in Election Code Section 18.

     The Board of Directors of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District
are directly elected by "voters" who meet the definition provided
in Election Code Section 18.  Thus, the Board is exempt from the
provisions of Section 84308 when it acts in its entirety as
itself, or when a committee, designated by the Board and composed
solely of members of the Board, acts.

     I hope this clarifies this matter for you.  If you have
any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at
916/322-5901.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                Janis Shank McLean
                                                Janis Shank McLean
                                                Counsel
                                                Legal Division

JSM:km

*Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the
Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

BART                    BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT
                                 800 Madison Street
                                 Oakland, California 94607
                                 Telephone (415) 465-4100


                                 February 9, 1983


ROBERT S. ALLEN
    PRESIDENT           Barbara Milman, Esq.
                        Fair Political Practices Commission
JOHN H. KIRKWOOD        P.O. Box 807
    VICE PRESIDENT      Sacramento, California 95804

KEITH BERNARD
    GENERAL MANAGER     re Chapter 1049 Statutes of 1982
                           (Section 84308 of the California Government Code)

                        Dear Ms. Milman:

DIRECTORS
                        As a result of my conversation of February 8, 1983,
BARCLAY SIMPSON        with Ms. Janice McClean of your office, I understand
    1ST DISTRICT       that you prefer presentation of our question con-
                        cerning California Government Code Section 84308 and
NELLO BIANCO           its application to BART Directors to be in writing.
    2ND DISTRICT

ARTHUR J. SMARTSIS     The Board of Directors of the San Francisco Bay Area
    3RD DISTRICT       Rapid Transit District is composed of nine individual
                        directors elected from nine districts within San
MARGARET K. PRYOR      Francisco, Alameda, and Contra Costa Counties.  The
    4TH DISTRICT       authorizing statutes for BART are found in Public
                        Utilities Codes Sections 28500, et. seq., and the
ROBERT S. ALLEN        provisions specifying the election of the District's
    5TH DISTRICT       Board of Directors are found in Sections 28745, etc.
                        seq.
JOHN GLENN
    6TH DISTRICT

WILFRED T. USSERY      The District's Board of Directors meets bi-monthly
    7TH DISTRICT       as a full body and also meets more frequently in
                        several committees comprised of various members of
EUGENE GARFINKLE      the board.  The function of the committees are ad-
    8TH DISTRICT       visory only and matters of policy and other actions
                        taken by the Board of Directors are taken at meetings
JOHN H. KIRKWOOD       of the full board.
    9TH DISTRICT

                        Among the matters which come before the Board of
                        Directors for approval are lease agreements, conces-
                        sion permits and other requests for use of District
                        property.  With regard to these activities and
                        other similar activities which may come within the
                        heading "... an application for license, permit, or
                        other entitlement for use ..." as set forth in

Barbara Milman, Esq.
February 9, 1983
Page 2


Government Code Section 84308, I would appreciate your advice
concerning the following questions:

     1.     Are the provisions of Chapter 1049 applicable to
the BART Board of Directors where they are considering such
things as leases, concession permits, and other uses of
District property.

     2.     Is the District included among the legislative
bodies referred to in Subparagraph D of Section 84308?

     3.     If the District is included within the language of
Subparagraph D of Section 84308, are the District's Board of
Directors exempt from the provisions of Chapter 1049 when,
while acting as a body or committee thereof, actions are
taken by the Board approving or otherwise acting upon appli-
cations for such things as leases, concession permits or
uses of District property.

Thank you very much for your cooperation in this matter.

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        MALCOLM BARRETT
                                        General Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        John Carroll Naish
                                        Attorney

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • •• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901         322-6441           322-6444


February 15, 1983


John Carroll Naisn
Attorney at Law
Bay Area Rapid Transit Dist.
800 Madison Street
Oakland, CA  94607

                                                    Re: A-83-041

Dear Mr. Naisn:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLean, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

BAM:km

embedding.qa_text: February 25, 1983 Mr. John Carrol Naish Bay Area Rapid Transit District 800 Madison Street Oakland, CA 94607 Re: Your Request for Advice, Our Advice No. A-83-041 Dear Mr. Naish: Thank you for your request for advice on Government Code Section 84308* (AB 1040). Your question is whether, and to what extent, the provisions of this section apply to the members of the Board of Directors of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District. Section 84308(d) exempts "legislative bodies ... while acting as a body or committee thereof" from the coverage of this section. A commission or board qualifies as a "legislative body" only if its entire membership is directly elected by the voters to serve on that body. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1(a).) The term "voters" is defined in Election Code Section 18. The Board of Directors of the Bay Area Rapid Transit District are directly elected by "voters" who meet the definition provided in Election Code Section 18. Thus, the Board is exempt from the provisions of Section 84308 when it acts in its entirety as itself, or when a committee, designated by the Board and composed solely of members of the Board, acts. I hope this clarifies this matter for you. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901. Very truly yours, Janis Shank McLean Janis Shank McLean Counsel Legal Division JSM:km *Hereinafter all statutory references made are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. February 9, 1983 Barbara Milman, Esq. Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, California 95804 re Chapter 1049 Statutes of 1982 (Section 84308 of the California Government Code) Dear Ms. Milman: As a result of my conversation of February 8, 1983, with Ms. Janice McClean of your office, I understand that you prefer presentation of our question concerning California Government Code Section 84308 and its application to BART Directors to be in writing. The Board of Directors of the San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District is composed of nine individual directors elected from nine districts within San Francisco, Alameda, and Contra Costa Counties. The authorizing statutes for BART are found in Public Utilities Codes Sections 28500, et seq., and the provisions specifying the election of the District's Board of Directors are found in Sections 28745, et seq. The District's Board of Directors meets bi-monthly as a full body and also meets more frequently in several committees comprised of various members of the Board. The function of the committees are advisory only and matters of policy and other actions taken by the Board of Directors are taken at meetings of the full Board. Among the matters which come before the Board of Directors for approval are lease agreements, concession permits and other requests for use of District property. With regard to these activities and other similar activities which may come within the heading "... an application for license, permit, or other entitlement for use ..." as set forth in Barbara Milman, Esq. February 9, 1983
